headline: CCL, RCL, NCLH Stocks Slammed as Oil Prices Surge and Geopolitical Tensions Roil Cruise Sector
summary: Shares of Carnival (NYSE:CCL) are getting hit in midday trading on Monday, sitting at $24.72, down nearly 3% on the session with an intraday low of $23.47. This caps a brutal stretch: -15% over the past week and -26.78% over the past month. The entire cruise sector is under pressure, but CCL is taking the ... headline: Hims partners with Novo Nordisk, Live Nation settles antitrust suit
summary: Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman takes a look at some of Monday morning's trending tickers and stories, including Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) to start selling Novo Nordisk's (NVO) GLP-1 weight-loss drugs on its platform, Live Nation (LYV) settling its antitrust lawsuit with the Department of Justice (DOJ), and cruise line stocks taking a hit from escalations in the Iran war. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

summary: American Airlines receives downgrade. Surging oil, jet fuel prices from Iran and Middle East conflicts create 'existential threat' for airlines.

headline: The Winners and Losers of Monday's Selloff
summary: Cruise operators, airlines and bank stocks fell in premarket trading Monday, while energy companies rallied.  As markets brace for a more protracted conflict in the Middle East, investors are rushing to identify the winners and losers from the most severe shock to energy markets since the 1970s.  Travel companies are leading losses in premarket U.S. trading.

headline: Airline and cruise stocks plunge as oil prices surge on Iran conflict
summary: Investing.com -- United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) shares fell 6% on Monday, while American Airlines (NASDAQ:AAL) dropped 5%, Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) declined 4.5%, and Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) slipped 3.8% as companies exposed to high oil prices sold off following a surge in crude prices.
